name: Sodium Hydroxide (NaOH), pellets
striking_fact: NaOH pellets can absorb enough moisture from humid air to dissolve themselves in minutes — weigh quickly and cap immediately.

hazard_statements: H290: May be corrosive to metals
H314: Causes severe skin burns and eye damage

sds_facts.storage: Store in a sealed, airtight container in a cool, dry, well-ventilated area. Keep away from moisture, acids, and metals. Desiccated storage recommended.

bench_knowledge: NaOH is deliquescent — it absorbs water from air and liquefies. Work quickly: pour pellets directly onto the scale, weigh, and cap the stock bottle immediately. In high-humidity environments, weigh over dry ice or in a desiccator.
Weigh by difference when working in humid conditions: note the full bottle weight, remove pellets, reweigh. This avoids accumulated error from moisture gain during the weighing process.
Store in a desiccator or with desiccant packets inside the stock bottle cap. Write the date of first opening on the bottle; significantly moisture-contaminated NaOH has a lower effective molarity.
Dissolution in water is highly exothermic — add NaOH slowly to water (never water to NaOH) and stir continuously. Always add base to water, never the reverse.

PP (polypropylene) and HDPE are compatible for NaOH up to ~10 M at room temperature. Glass is fine but can etch slowly with prolonged contact with strong base; borosilicate is more resistant than soda-lime.
When preparing 10 N NaOH (400 g/L), the solution gets hot (~80°C); prepare in a heat-resistant container, cool completely before pH measurement — NaOH electrodes are affected by temperature.
